title: Broiled Tomato Gazpacho
description: A tangy, tender meal-prep Spanish dish featuring vine tomatoes that are briefly broiled to enhance their flavor and texture.
instructions: Preheat your broiler according to the manufacturer's instructions. Make sure you have a baking sheet ready.
Wash and dry 4 medium-sized vine tomatoes thoroughly. Cut them in half, but leave some of their skin attached for easier handling.
Place the tomato halves cut side up on the prepared baking sheet. Drizzle with 2 tablespoons of olive oil evenly over each half.
Sprinkle 1/4 teaspoon of red pepper flakes (if using) over each tomato half to add a touch of heat and flavor. Season with salt and black pepper to taste, if desired.
Place under the preheated broiler until the tomatoes begin to brown slightly on the bottom side (about 5-7 minutes). Rotate them halfway through for even cooking.
Once done, remove from the oven and set aside to cool for a few minutes. Slice each tomato into halves or quarters if desired, removing any seeds and stems.
Chop the cooled tomatoes finely and place them in a large bowl. Drizzle with additional olive oil and red pepper flakes, then toss gently until evenly coated.
Garnish with chopped fresh dill before serving. Serve cold as a refreshing side dish or use it as a base for your meal prep.
